name: aside-container
role: Outer container. Sets the data-semantic attribute (note / tip / warning / danger / success / info) which drives token mapping for surface, border, icon, and aria-label.

registerContracts.inline: Minimal — icon + label inline with body; no surface fill, just an inline-start accent border.
registerContracts.bordered: Surface fill + inline-start accent border in the semantic colour. The default register; works in prose flow.
registerContracts.filled: Fully filled surface in the semantic-soft colour. Use for higher-emphasis callouts; avoid stacking multiple in close succession.

meta.roleNote: Default role='note'. For semantically critical content where AT should announce it more prominently, operators may switch to role='alert' (typically for danger semantic only). Operators choose explicitly; the pack does NOT auto-promote to role='alert'.

name: aside-icon
role: Decorative icon that signals the semantic register. Always aria-hidden. The semantic colour comes from token mapping; the glyph itself is chosen by the consumer (the pack does not ship icons inline — that's the consumer's icon-pack responsibility).

meta.ariaHidden: Always aria-hidden='true'. The aside-container's aria-label carries the semantic signal for AT users; the icon is purely visual reinforcement.
meta.iconChoiceContract: The pack defines token mapping (icon colour comes from the semantic-accent token) but does NOT ship the icon glyphs. Consumers wire icons from an icon pack (e.g., @quoin/icons-lucide, @quoin/icons-phosphor) or inline SVG. Recommended glyph mapping: note=info-circle, tip=lightbulb, warning=triangle-alert, danger=octagon-alert, success=check-circle, info=info-circle.

name: aside-label
role: Short heading or label for the aside. Optional but recommended — gives context to the body. Visually styled in the semantic-accent colour.

meta.headingLevelChoice:
When the aside is inside an article and the surrounding section uses h3, set level='h4' to keep the heading hierarchy clean. Default is

with strong typography — explicit heading semantics are opt-in.
meta.redundancyWithAriaLabel: The label text is visual; the aside-container's aria-label gives AT users a semantic label. The two are not duplicative — the label is the human-authored title (e.g., 'Heads up'), the aria-label is the semantic role ('Warning').

name: aside-content
role: The body of the aside. Flow content — may contain paragraphs, lists, code blocks, links. Token styling inherits from prose surroundings.

meta.inheritedTypography: Paragraph + list + link rendering inherits from the surrounding prose context. The aside doesn't re-declare prose tokens; if the consumer hasn't set them, the baseline serves.
